description: A Gantt chart: one GanttBar per row-hierarchy member (Category > Sub-Category > Product Name > Order ID) laid out over a Year/Month date on cols, its length sized by a duration calc (Ship Lag = DATEDIFF of two dates) and colored by a status calc (Ship Lag Indicator) — a schedule/duration view over time. Lane W11-F2: the three source filter pins (product-name list, sub-category, year) were PARAMETERIZED (collapsed to level-members) so those fields stay data-driven Filters pills with no source literals.

slots.notes: Outermost row grouping of the schedule hierarchy.
Second row grouping, nested under Category.
Third row grouping, nested under Sub-Category.
Leaf row grain — one Gantt bar per member (the individual task).
Year part of the SAME bound start-date field (outer cols axis). Reuses Order Date at a second derivation — see hazard single-date-two-derivations.
Month part of the SAME bound start-date field (inner cols axis).

The END date. Not on a shelf; it feeds the Ship Lag duration calc (DATEDIFF of Order Date -> Ship Date). A DISTINCT second date field is required — with the single-date fixture only one date exists, so fixture_bind=false (see hazard single-date-two-derivations).

calcs.notes: Ship Lag — the duration (bar length/size) between the start (Order Date) and end (Ship Date). Requires both bindable date slots.

calcs.formula:
If [Calculation_291608117815463936] >= 5 then "Late"
ElseIf [Calculation_291608117815463936] >=2 Then "On-Time"
Else "Early"
END
calcs.notes: Ship Lag Indicator — buckets the duration into Late/On-Time/Early for the bar color. Template-internal: its only input is the Ship Lag calc, not a bindable slot.

hazards.detail: The source workbook pinned Product Name to a nine-member product list. Lane F2's compile-time filter policy PARAMETERIZED it: the member union was collapsed to the neutral all-members form (<groupfilter function='level-members' level='[none:Product Name:nk]'/>), so Product Name stays a data-driven Filters-shelf pill (its <slices> entry preserved) with the source literals removed.

The source workbook pinned Sub-Category to member='"Chairs"'. PARAMETERIZED: collapsed to <groupfilter function='level-members' level='[none:Sub-Category:nk]'/>, so Sub-Category stays a data-driven Filters-shelf pill with the source literal removed.

The source workbook pinned Year of Order Date to a {2016, 2026} member list. PARAMETERIZED: collapsed to <groupfilter function='level-members' level='[yr:Order Date:ok]'/>, so Year stays a data-driven Filters-shelf pill with the source literals removed — the same neutral form the apply-path 3c neutralizer writes (idempotent).

The start date [Order Date] is used at BOTH Year and Month on cols, AND the Ship Lag duration calc needs a DISTINCT second date [Ship Date]. Against the single-date committed fixture these extra temporal slots cannot bind distinct fields, so fixture_bind=false — a valid, honest state (same convention as change-over-time-calendar-heatmap). On a real dataset the Order Date derivations bind the same field via qualified keys and Ship Date binds a second date; the false reflects the fixture's one-date shape, not a template defect.
